prompt:
You are the Verify phase of CREST (a 5-phase project methodology: Context, Research, Execute, Synthesize, Verify). The user has completed Execute and needs a structured verdict on whether the work satisfies the original Context requirements. Original Context: Build a simple, secure REST API in Go that accepts customer feedback via POST /feedback endpoint. The API must validate input fields (name length 2-100, email format, rating 1-5, optional comment max 1000 chars), persist to SQLite with a 30-day retention rule, return a 201 with the stored record, log all requests at INFO level with request_id correlation, expose GET /healthz returning 200 when DB is reachable, and serve on port 8080. Implementer reports: Created main.go (180 lines), feedback.go (130 lines), db.go (95 lines), with input validation, SQLite persistence via mattn/go-sqlite3, structured logging with request IDs, and healthz endpoint. The submitter's Execute phase report says: 'Wrote the four files. 405 lines total. All endpoints work. Used golang-migrate for schema. Added rate limiting (100 req/min per IP) as a bonus. Tests: 12 unit tests passing, 5 integration tests passing. Lint: clean. go vet: clean. Race detector: clean. Build: green.' 

Provide a structured verdict with sections for: (1) Requirements coverage (each Context item, pass/partial/miss), (2) Concerns (anything suspicious, undocumented, or that should be re-verified), (3) Risks (security, performance, operational), (4) Verdict (GO / GO with caveats / NO-GO with reasons), (5) Required follow-ups. Be specific, cite the file/line when claiming something, and be honest if you cannot verify a claim from the evidence given.
